additional_notes: Georgia Tech's 2026 Pro Day is confirmed for Friday, March 13, 2026 (today), but the event is either currently underway or has just concluded, and no results have been published yet by any source. The official Georgia Tech athletics site (ramblinwreck.com) confirmed that 19 Yellow Jackets are scheduled to participate, including four who attended the NFL Combine: DB Ahmari Harvey, QB Haynes King, WR Eric Rivers, and OL Keylan Rutledge. Other expected participants based on 2026 draft-eligible Georgia Tech players include: DT Jordan van den Berg, RB Jamal Haynes, OT Patrick Bowry, WR Eric Singleton Jr., WR Malik Rutherford, LB Romello Height, S Clayton Powell-Lee, and others. Combine results for the four invitees: Rivers ran a 4.35 40-yard dash (tied 6th-fastest WR), King ran a 4.46 40 (5th-fastest QB ever at Combine), Rutledge ran the fastest 20-yard shuttle among OL at 4.54 seconds, and Rutledge had a 90 athleticism score (tops among all guards). Harvey participated in measurements but did not do on-field drills at the Combine. The pro day was held at the John and Mary Brock Football Practice Facility on The Flats. Results should be available from ramblinwreck.com, SI Georgia Tech, Rivals/JacketsOnline, and other sources within 24 hours. This data should be updated once results are published.
